mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and rising household savings indicates demand overheating and shifting consumption patterns, prompting market repricing of near-term equity valuations downward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and RSAFS move together, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which generates positive short-term momentum in equity markets during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of recovery regime observations, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.1 million units signal robust construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering positive equity returns over the following month as capital flows into cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 1100.0 fail to precede positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive equity returns as consumer spending capacity expands.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative or flat forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation during a low-rate environment forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases during low-rate periods fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the mechanism loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%.
